patches.find: a field-specific published dataset published MT and ELF spectral literature that was publicly available
patches.replace: a field-specific published dataset (published MT and ELF spectral literature) that was publicly available
patches.rationale: Grammar bug introduced by ISS-1867 patch: two noun phrases concatenated without connector. Restores parenthetical pattern matching WIN-008 and 17 other post-hoc-paragraph WINs. Self-applying: clear minor prose fix, no verdict change.

patches.find: that scale precisely with each star's distance
patches.replace: that scale with each star's distance to within projection geometry
patches.rationale: NH parallax ratio 32.4/15.7=2.064 vs distance ratio 7.86/4.24=1.854 — 11% discrepancy. 'Precisely' overclaims. Replacement is accurate and removes defender attack surface. Self-applying: text-only, no verdict change.

patches.find: href="https://www.nasa.gov/solar-system/nasas-new-horizons-conducts-the-first-interstellar-parallax-experiment/" target="_blank">Throop et al. 2020, Research Notes AAS 4, 74
patches.replace: href="https://doi.org/10.3847/2515-5172/ab9088" target="_blank">Throop et al. 2020, Research Notes AAS 4, 74
patches.rationale: Citation link text says 'Throop et al. 2020, Research Notes AAS 4, 74' but URL points to NASA press release, not the cited journal. DOI 10.3847/2515-5172/ab9088 resolves to IOP Science RNAAS. Symmetry fix — we criticize dome for citation mismatches. Self-applying: URL-only fix, no text or verdict change.

patches.find: rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation) shows the same 1/d scaling
patches.replace: rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation; even if spectroscopic M_V calibrations carry residual parallax dependence at the Hyades-anchored level, the dome wobble theory predicts flat amplitude regardless of which rung the calibration sits on, so the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung) shows the same 1/d scaling
patches.rationale: Spectroscopic-class calibration is itself Hyades-parallax-anchored. Without this clause a dome defender can argue we pushed parallax dependence one rung down, not eliminated it. The added clause closes that structural escape hatch. Self-applying: additive clause, no verdict change.

patches.find: well outside the dome's own confirmation thresholds (~5%)
patches.replace: well outside the single-digit residuals the dome treats as confirmatory at WIN-029
patches.rationale: TLDR implies dome published a 5% threshold it never actually published. Detail caveats this but TLDR is what most readers see. Replacement cites specific WIN-029 example without fabricating a general dome rule. Self-applying: minor text fix, no verdict change.

Finding field's '5.3% threshold' phrasing attributes a published threshold rule to the dome that doesn't exist. Replacing with 'WIN-029 confirmation residual' makes clear we cite one specific example, not a general dome standard. Self-applying: minor text fix, no verdict change.
